query: Swiss apricot tart Aprikosenwaehe with custard filling

draft.title: Aprikosenwähe
draft.description: Swiss apricot tart with a tender shortcrust base and creamy custard filling, topped with fresh apricot halves. A classic dessert perfect for summer.

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.note: 50 g for the crust, 75 g for the custard

ingredients.name: egg
ingredients.note: 1 for the crust, 2 for the custard

steps.text: Prepare the shortcrust pastry by mixing flour, sugar, and cold butter until crumbly, then add one egg and knead into a smooth dough. Wrap and chill for 30 minutes if possible.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Roll out the chilled dough on a floured surface and line a 26 cm tart pan with removable bottom. Prick the base with a fork.
Blind bake the crust for 10 minutes with baking paper and baking beans or rice to prevent rising.
Remove the baking paper and beans, then bake the crust for another 5 minutes until lightly golden.
In a bowl, whisk eggs, sugar, vanilla extract, and cornstarch until smooth. Warm milk and cream in a saucepan until just below boiling, then slowly whisk into the egg mixture.
Pour the custard mixture back into the saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly until it thickens. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Arrange apricot halves cut side up on the baked tart crust.
Pour the custard evenly over the apricots.
Bake the tart at 180°C (350°F) for 20 minutes until the custard is set and the apricots are tender.
Let the Aprikosenwähe cool before removing from the pan and serving.
